title: Affirming the nature and importance of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization and Article 5 commitments under the North Atlantic Treaty.
plain_summary: This resolution shows that the House of Representatives strongly supports NATO, which is a group of countries that promise to help defend each other. It says the United States will keep its promise to protect NATO allies if they are attacked, which is called Article 5. The resolution also supports letting new countries like Ukraine choose to join NATO if they want to. It encourages all NATO countries to spend at least 2% of their money on defense to stay strong against threats from countries like Russia, China, and Iran.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution affirms the nature and importance of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization and Article 5 commitments under the North Atlantic Treaty.

sections.title: Background Statements
sections.text: The United States and our democratic allies face serious international challenges and new threats to world security. The North Atlantic Treaty Organization, also called NATO, was created on April fourth, nineteen forty-nine. It was formed to stop Soviet expansion, prevent more world wars in Europe, and build a commitment to security across the Atlantic Ocean. NATO is built on democratic principles of freedom, security, and the right of nations to govern themselves. Article 5 of the North Atlantic Treaty supports the idea of collective defense, which means if one member is attacked, all members help defend it. This has been a guiding principle of United States foreign policy for more than seventy-five years. The United States commitment to Article 5 collective defense helps deter enemies like Russia, China, and Iran that want to spread their harmful influence. When allies unite under Article 5 commitments, they create stronger defense against threats to their collective safety and the stability of democratic countries. Working together across the Atlantic through NATO acts as a strong barrier against the spread of harmful influence, dangerous technologies, and destabilizing operations from enemies. Authoritarian governments like Russia, China, Iran, and North Korea have increased their cooperation in political, economic, and security areas. They seek to undermine democratic principles and create distrust in democratic institutions. The only time Article 5 was used was after the September eleventh, two thousand one attacks. NATO allies provided troops, intelligence, resources, and support to United States forces. NATO allies suffered significant casualties during NATO-led operations in Afghanistan. NATO has been steadfast in supporting Ukraine during Russia's full-scale invasion. Member countries have provided ammunition, weapons, and significant non-security assistance. This has increased Ukraine's ability to defend itself and imposed direct costs on Russia for its illegal invasion. After Vladimir Putin's illegal invasion of Ukraine, Finland and Sweden made the independent decision to join the NATO alliance. NATO remains committed to addressing the systematic challenges posed by China to Euro-Atlantic interests and security. NATO member states have continued to strengthen and develop capabilities in advanced defense and cyber technologies. This includes the Defense Innovation Accelerator for the North Atlantic program, which includes cooperation with leading researchers and innovators.

sections.title: House Resolution
sections.text: The House of Representatives reaffirms full and unwavering commitment to the North Atlantic Treaty Organization. The House restates the importance of NATO and the vital commitment of the United States to the alliance and to defending United States allies under Article 5 of the North Atlantic Treaty. The House reaffirms NATO's open door policy and commitment to Article 10 of the North Atlantic Treaty. The House reaffirms that every country has the right to choose its own security arrangements, including Ukraine. The House remembers the thousands of NATO coalition soldiers who sacrificed their lives in support of the United States following the use of Article 5 after the September eleventh, two thousand one terrorist attacks against the United States. The House underscores the importance of all NATO allies dedicating at least two percent of their gross domestic product to their national defense or establishing plans by the Washington Summit to meet their two percent obligations. Gross domestic product means the total value of all goods and services produced by a country in one year. The House highlights the importance of coordination and collaboration in advanced defense technologies, counter-intelligence, and cybersecurity programs among NATO allies given the evolving threats posed by adversaries such as Russia, China, and Iran.
